commands.review_summary:
The PR narrows Codex app-server memory flush runs to OpenClaw-managed read/write tools, disables native Code Mode and sandbox shell re-addition for those runs, and adds a regression test.

Reproducibility: yes. Current source shows memory-triggered runs reach the Codex app-server path without a memory-specific native/sandbox shell guard, while the shared tool factory contract restricts memory flushes to read and append-only write.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer draft needs normal maintainer review and merge gating; no narrow automated repair was identified.

target.title: [codex] fix codex memory flush tool surface
target.body:
## What changed

- Disable the Codex app-server native Code Mode tool surface for memory-triggered flush runs.
- Keep Codex memory flush dynamic tools narrowed to OpenClaw-managed `read` and append-only `write`.
- Prevent sandbox shell dynamic tools from being added back during memory flush runs.
- Add a regression test that verifies memory flush metadata is forwarded and that `exec`, `process`, `apply_patch`, and `message` are not exposed.

## Why

The observed Telegram failure came from a Codex memory flush turn using native shell access to run `tail -80 memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. The canonical memory file did not exist yet because the session-memory hook had written a timestamped file, so the internal tool call failed visibly.

Memory flushes already rely on OpenClaw's restricted append-only write guard in the shared tool factory. Codex was bypassing that intent because native Code Mode still exposed shell and file tools. This change makes Codex memory flush behavior match the restricted OpenClaw surface.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "limits Codex memory flush|points yielded sandbox_exec"`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Known unrelated test drift

A full run of `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` currently fails two image-cleanup tests because current behavior emits an extra `thread/unsubscribe` request while the tests expect only `thread/start|resume` and `turn/start`. Those failures reproduce when selected by themselves and do not touch the memory flush path.
target.author: amknight
